description: Bootstrap integration: fresh appliance bootstrap generates exactly one claim token, stores it in the expected Kubernetes Secret, and prints a claim URL using the configured app URL.
DB integration (happy path): appliance claim token verification accepts a valid unexpired token by comparing against stored token hash.
DB integration (guard cases): expired, already-claimed, or unknown appliance claim tokens are rejected without mutating appliance state.
Server integration: appliance claim completion is blocked when appliance mode is disabled or when an MSP admin already exists.

DB-backed integration (happy path): redeeming a valid claim token on a fresh appliance creates the minimum tenant record(s), tenant settings, first MSP user, and MSP Admin role assignment in one successful flow.
DB-backed integration (race/guard): two redemption attempts for the same token result in only one created initial admin and one claimed token state.
UI route test: `/auth/appliance-claim` shows the claim form for a valid token and a clear terminal error state for invalid or already-used tokens.
Auth integration: a successful claim produces a valid MSP session and redirects the created admin to `/msp/onboarding`.
Manual appliance smoke test: operator can retrieve the claim token from the Kubernetes Secret after bootstrap output is lost and still complete the first-admin claim flow successfully.
